Registry / Terms / maximum permissible error

maximum permissible error

—Not in VIM/VIML G 18 #00361 · 7 instances

Suggested actions

  1. HIGH No authoritative definition in VIM or VIML.
  2. MEDIUM 5 distinct definitions — harmonise.

Publication instances

Ed.YearPublicationClauseDefinitionConsistency

#02327
3.29
extreme permitted value by the present Recommendation for the error of indication
pending

#01561
T.e.4.3
extreme value for an error permitted by this Recommendation
pending
20101975OIML R 24:1975
#00361
2.1
maximum error, positive or negative, allowed on Verification Officers' metre standard on initial or subsequent verification when compared at the reference temperature of 20 °C with the corresponding standard of length of higher accuracy (in the statutory hierarchy of standards), assuming that this standard of higher accuracy has a negligible error for the purpose of this comparison
pending
202X2022OIML R139-1:2022
#03231
3.1.5
extreme value of measurement error, with respect to a known reference quantity value, permitted by specifications or regulations for a given measurement, measuring instrument, or measuring system [OIML V2-200:2012, 4.26] [OIML V1:2013, 0.05]
pending

extreme permitted value by the present Recommendation for the error of indication

maximum error, positive or negative, allowed on Verification Officers’ metre standard on initial or subsequent verification when compared at the reference temperature of 20 °C with the corresponding standard of length of higher accuracy (in the statutory hierarchy of standards), assuming that this standard of higher accuracy has a negligible error for the purpose of this comparison
